.expect("root typed plan remains installed after gating");
.expect("root prompt installation must publish a typed plan");

preexisting_physical_oversize.status: disclosed residual; these files were already at or above the physical limit at the exact base, while their current AST-stripped production code remains below the limit

method.added_line_scope: scan added Rust lines after Rust-literal masking; line byte offsets are classified against AST-proven cfg(test) ranges, retaining test matches as permitted evidence instead of silently dropping them; supplemental rules cover UFCS unwrap/expect calls and cfg_attr lint suppressions
method.cfg_test: remove AST items whose cfg cannot be true with test=false
method.counter: tokei 14.0.0 compiled with serialization support: json, cbor, yaml
method.new_file_loc: every Rust file added in the exact diff is measured by physical lines and must remain strictly below 500, including test-only files
method.preexisting_oversize: compare final physical lines with the exact base blob; pre-existing oversized files are disclosed separately and do not weaken the strict new-file or AST-stripped production-code limits
method.production_loc: ast-grep removes items whose cfg cannot be true with test=false; tokei counts the remaining Rust code and every changed file must be <500
method.split_bypass: all added Rust lines, including tests, are scanned for include! and #[path = ...]; neither is accepted as a file-size split

source_cleanliness.working_tree_note: non-Rust documentation edits may be present; this source-bound policy reads committed Rust at SOURCE and rejects any dirty/untracked Rust
